content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement · · Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660         322-5901         322-6441         322-6444

November 26, 1980


J. Wilmar Jensen
Jensen, Gaarde & Brereton
1514 H Street
Modesto, CA 95354

Dear Mr. Jensen:

     Thank you for your very articulate letter on several
areas of campaigning with which you have some concern.
Members of the staff and the Commission have expressed
similar concerns over the six year period we have been in
existence.

     Enclosed are copies of reports we have issued on
"Transfers of Campaign Money" and "Personal Use of Campaign
Funds." Last year the Commission sponsored legislation
(AB 1168, enclosed) which would have prohibited use of cam-
paign funds for personal use. It was defeated on the
Assembly floor.

     The Commission has decided not to sponsor legislation pro-
hibiting transfers of funds. The enclosed report details some
of the reasons, but essentially we believe there is no way
this process can be stopped. For example, in the past elec-
tion, contributions were "transferred" by asking contributors
to make contributions directly to other candidates. In such
situations, there is even less disclosure - than if the money
is transferred directly by one candidate to another.

     As to loans, we treat them exactly like contributions
since in most cases personal loans are not repaid if the
candidate loses. There are no limitations on either loans or
contributions in state campaigns although some local juris-
dictions do have such limitations.

     I would be pleased to talk to you further about your con-
cerns. Please contact me at (916) 322-6441 if you would like
to continue our dialogue.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert M. Stern
                                        General Counsel

RMS:nc
Enclosures

LAW OFFICES
JENSEN, GAARDE & BRERETON
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

J. WILMAR JENSEN, INC.
RALPH E. GAARDE
HUGH E. BRERETON

OAKDALE OFFICES
128 SOUTH THIRD AVENUE
(209) 847-4230

1514 H STREET
MODESTO, CALIFORNIA 95354

NEWMAN OFFICES
1031 FRESNO STREET
(209) 862-2846

(209) 529-0791

November 10, 1980

Executive Secretary
Fair Political Practices
Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Gentlemen:

I am wondering what rationale there is in the law or the rules
and regulations of your Commission which permits one political
candidate to collect moneys for his candidacy; and then transfer
those moneys to another candidate in another District for
another position? We have many gross examples before us in
the Primary and in the General Election. For example, it
would hardly have been practicable for either Assemblyman
McCarthy or Assemblyman Berman to carry on their warfare if
this sort of procedure was illegal.

To me, this procedure promotes bossism, removes some of the
responsibility that a candidate would feel to his constituents,
creates dependance on other political figures, eliminates
independance on the part of our political leaders and is totally
unnecessary to make the process of government work.

I further understand that the law permits unexpended funds
remaining in a political war chest, when one's days in government cease, to be used for the personal benefit of the candidate
himself. I do not advocate the return of these funds to the
individual contributors but at least in the case of members
of our two major parties, those funds should be payable to
the respective Central Committees for the use of those bodies
in the discharge of their responsibilities to the Party.

I am not certain of the regulations regarding loans. I constantly
read of loans which are far in excess of limitations otherwise
imposed upon gifts. Further, I know of no requirement that the
loans be interest bearing. Finally, I am convinced that many of
these loans are never repaid.

Page 2
November 10, 1980

Executive Secretary
Fair Political Practices Commission


I believe that this is an area where substantial abuse can
occur. Rules should be developed to tighten up on areas of most
probable impropriety.

I should appreciate any thoughts that any one might have regarding
these matters. I believe they deserve some attention, but I
am frank to admit that I am not well acquainted with all of the
rules and regulations and provisions of the law. There may,
perhaps, be provisions of which I am not aware which ameliorate
some of my comments. In that event, I would like to know what
they are.

Might I hear from you.

Very truly yours,

JENSEN, GAARDE & BRERETON

J. Wilmar Jensen

JWJ:dr
